rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical decarboxylative sp2-sp3 cross-coupling of NHPI redox-active esters with vinyl iodides using a Ni/bpy catalyst on Ag-nanoparticle-functionalized RVC cathode with sacrificial Mg anode
electrochemical_role: Cathodic reduction of Ag(I) (from AgNO3 + halide) deposits Ag nanoparticles on RVC; the Ag-NP-modified cathode then reduces Ni(II)(bpy) to lower-valent Ni species that activates the NHPI redox-active ester (RAE) to an alkyl radical (with loss of CO2 and phthalimide), which is captured by a Ni-alkenyl species formed from oxidative addition of the vinyl iodide; sacrificial Mg anode is oxidized to Mg(2+)

key_intermediates.name: Ag nanoparticles on RVC cathode (functionalized electrode)
Ni(III)(bpy)(alkenyl)(alkyl) intermediate prior to reductive elimination
Ni(II)(bpy)(alkenyl) halide (after oxidative addition of vinyl iodide)
Over-reduced Ni(0) / aggregated Ni(I) (catalyst deactivation, passivating film)

electron_flow: Initial cathodic charge (0–0.3 F/mol) reduces Ag(I) (generated in situ from AgNO3 + halide) to Ag(0) nanoparticles on the RVC cathode; subsequently the Ag-NP cathode reduces Ni(II)(bpy) to Ni(I), which fragments the NHPI redox-active ester into an alkyl radical, CO2, and phthalimide, while a separate Ni(II)-alkenyl (from oxidative addition of vinyl iodide) intercepts the radical and undergoes reductive elimination to forge the sp2-sp3 bond; Mg(0) anode supplies the balancing oxidation.
rate_determining_step: Not explicitly identified; induction period is governed by Ag(I) reduction/deposition prior to productive cross-coupling

probe_reagents.name: AgNO3 omission control
probe_reagents.observation: Without AgNO3, yield drops to 20%; with 0.3 equiv AgNO3 yield rises to 52%, confirming Ag-NP role

probe_reagents.name: LiCl additive / halide source control
probe_reagents.observation: Halide required to form photosensitive AgCl; without LiCl, NiCl2 can serve as halide donor; prolonged stir before electrolysis decomposes AgCl photochemically and lowers yield to 17%

probe_reagents.name: Pure Ag electrode / Ag-plated electrode substitution
probe_reagents.observation: Bulk Ag electrodes performed poorly; Ag-NP on RVC was essential, indicating nanoparticulate Ag (not bulk) is the active surface

probe_reagents.name: Stoichiometric Mg reductant (no current)
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction does not proceed efficiently without electrolysis even with Mg reductant; electrochemistry is essential

probe_reagents.name: Benzyl chloride redox probe at Ag-NP vs bare GC electrode
probe_reagents.observation: 370 mV positive shift of BnCl reduction on Ag-NP-modified electrode, confirming Ag-NP lowers overpotential

probe_reagents.name: Methyl ester vinyl iodide 40 vs free-acid vinyl iodide 5 (RDE comparison)
probe_reagents.observation: ECcat current observed with 40 (no passivation in presence of RAE); with acid 5, a second reduction wave at -1.48 V persists even with RAE, attributed to Ni(II/III)-alkenyl reduction to Ni(I)-alkenyl that deactivates via dimerization

notes: Authors propose four distinct roles for the Ag-NP layer: (I) prevents catalyst adsorption/passivation, (II) lowers cathode overpotential (-1.15 V vs -1.66 V without AgNO3, a 510 mV shift) keeping it above Ni-catalyst decomposition potential, (III) slows diffusion of RAE to the electrode (Levich analysis shows 2x lower diffusion coefficient on Ag-NP electrode), and (IV) prevents sequential over-reduction of Ni(II/III)-alkenyl to deactivated Ni(I)-alkenyl. Electrode surface characterized by SEM/TEM/EDS showing 10–100 nm Ag nanoparticles. Alternative role of bulk silver was ruled out by control with pure Ag electrodes. A comproportionation pathway between Ni(III) and Ni(I) species is also drawn in the proposed cycle.
